red_failure_evidence: Pre-fix, on the committed cells.mjs: reopenCell (and setTier/resetCellBudget/recordJudgeVerdict) had NO assertNotArchived guard at all. Repro: archiveFeature moved cell 'red-1' out of .bee/cells/ into the archive tree (active file confirmed gone via fs.existsSync). Calling reopenCell(root, 'red-1', 'rework needed') did NOT throw — it silently wrote a BRAND-NEW active-path file with status 'open', resurrecting the archived cell as a live duplicate that would fork from its archived twin on any subsequent mutation. Separately, writeCell had no serialization at all against a live archiveFeature/unarchiveFeature transaction (no 'cells-archive' lock acquire), and neither archiveFeature nor unarchiveFeature wrote a recovery journal or preflighted destination collisions before renaming, so a process kill mid-loop left a half-migrated feature with no way to detect or repair it on the next call.
tests_inspected: skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cells.mjs cells-archive-1/hardening-1 sections (existing archive-fallback and mutate-refusal tests for updateCell/claimCell/capCell).
tests_added_or_changed: 8 rows in test_cells.mjs: (1) repurposed the old EISDIR mid-loop-rollback test into a preflight-collision test (the old fixture is now caught before any rename); (2) the 4 newly guarded mutators refuse an archived cell; (3) writeCell throws CELL_ARCHIVED when called directly on an archived-only id; (4) writeCell throws CELLS_ARCHIVE_BUSY while the cells-archive lock is held (verified via a manually-acquired lock + release, then a follow-up write proving it succeeds once free); (5) unarchiveFeature refuses an active-destination collision; (6) archiveFeature journal-recovery test simulating a half-archive (files split between active/archive, journal on disk, no in-process rollback ever ran) recovers and then archives cleanly; (7) the symmetric unarchiveFeature journal-recovery test.
verification_run: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cells.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cli_cells.mjs -> 98 passed/0 failed, then 24 passed/0 failed, exit 0. Also spot-checked test_misc.mjs (recordJudgeVerdict/setTier callers) still green post-change (92 passed; its 1 unrelated failure is a pre-existing templates<->`.bee/bin` mirror byte-identity check, expected to be red mid-swarm across many concurrently-edited files and owned by the D9 close-out cell, not this cell).
